VAL-PROC-001.evidence: grep -c 'SAFETY' system/kernel/address_space.rs returned 12 (>= 10 required). Break-before-make and AddressSpace Drop were previously fixed and are present in the diff history.
VAL-PROC-001.notes: 12 SAFETY comments found, exceeding the 10-site minimum.

VAL-PROC-002.evidence: git diff origin/main -- system/kernel/process.rs system/kernel/process_exit.rs shows: (1) AUDIT comment at top of process.rs documenting 1 unsafe block verified, integer overflow bugs found/fixed (checked_page_count, checked_vma_end helpers added), process slot leak documented. (2) AUDIT comment at top of process_exit.rs confirming 0 unsafe blocks, two-phase wake pattern correct, no bugs found. (3) SAFETY comment on copy_segment_page expanded with full justification.
VAL-PROC-002.notes: Both files have AUDIT headers. Two overflow bugs fixed with checked arithmetic. process_exit.rs clean.

VAL-PROC-003.evidence: git diff origin/main -- system/kernel/address_space_id.rs system/kernel/executable.rs shows: (1) address_space_id.rs AUDIT comment confirming 1 unsafe block verified, wrapping/exhaustion behavior documented (bitmap 1..=255, generation u64, free-of-0 guard, double-free harmless). (2) executable.rs AUDIT comment confirming 0 unsafe blocks, bug found and fixed: file_size > mem_size bounds check added, stale comment about missing overflow checks removed.
VAL-PROC-003.notes: ASID exhaustion behavior fully documented. ELF file_size > mem_size bug found and fixed.

VAL-PROC-004.evidence: git diff origin/main --stat -- system/test/tests/ shows: executable.rs | 80 +++, process.rs | 116 +++. Both are new test files targeting process/address-space subsystem findings.
VAL-PROC-004.notes: 80 lines of executable tests and 116 lines of process tests added. Also paging.rs (+121) and mmio.rs (+138) added in related areas.
